ext.description: AtRef: copy the current file as @path:line for Claude Code / Cursor / Copilot — also Markdown links, with-code snippets (line numbers + context), absolute paths, multi-cursor.
cmd.copy.title: Copy (default format)
cmd.copyAsAtRef.title: Copy as @path:line
cmd.copyAsMarkdownLink.title: Copy as Markdown Link
cmd.copyAsColon.title: Copy as path:line
cmd.copyWithCode.title: Copy @path:line + Code Snippet
cmd.copyAbsolutePath.title: Copy Absolute Path with Line
menu.submenu.label: AtRef — Copy as…
cfg.defaultFormat.desc: Default format used by the main AtRef Copy command and the keybinding.
cfg.pathStyle.desc: How the path part is computed: relative to workspace, relative to git root, absolute, or just the file name.
cfg.rangeSeparator.desc: Separator between start and end lines for multi-line selections (e.g. '-' produces :45-60, ':' produces :45:60).
cfg.multiSelectionSeparator.desc: String used to join references when the editor has multiple non-empty selections. Default is a newline.
cfg.notification.desc: Where to show the 'Copied …' confirmation after a successful copy.
cfg.usePosixSeparators.desc: Force forward-slash path separators in the output (recommended; matches Claude/Cursor and Markdown links).
cfg.codeBlockLineNumbers.desc: Prefix each line in the '+ Code Snippet' code block with its original line number (recommended — lets the AI refer to a specific line unambiguously).
cfg.codeBlockContextLines.desc: Number of surrounding lines to include before/after the selection in '+ Code Snippet'. 0 = exact selection only. The @path:start-end header still reflects the original selection.
